plain_0: Every paying customer you refer = recurring revenue for you. No caps, no minimums.
text_1: Subject: The churn number sellers don't show you
plain_2: You're evaluating a SaaS acquisition. The seller says 2% monthly churn.
plain_3: Ask for the raw CSV. Run it through Churn Lens.
plain_4: I just ran one and found 9.4% real churn — a $340K gap on a $1M deal.
text_5: The tool is free to start: https://churnlens.site/get-the-checklist
plain_8: Most SaaS acquisition due diligence is theater. You trust a summary number the seller computed using their own methodology.
text_10: 80% of SaaS acquirers overpay because they trust the seller's churn number.
plain_11: I was one of them. The deal looked perfect on paper — 2.3% reported churn, growing MRR, sticky customer base.
plain_12: The real churn was 9.4%. The gap cost me $340K.
plain_13: Here's what I learned: there's no "industry standard" for churn calculation. Every seller chooses a methodology that flatters their number. The only fix is to compute it yourself from raw data.
plain_14: That's why I built Churn Lens — a buyer-side tool that catches hidden churn, concentration risk, and revenue decay sellers don't disclose.
text_16: No finance degree required. Just the CSV and 10 minutes.
text_17: Thinking of buying a SaaS business? Don't trust the churn number in the CIM.
plain_18: In this video, I show you the 23-point buyer-side churn audit that caught a $340K gap the seller didn't disclose.
plain_21: No minimums. No lock-in. Just great commissions and a tool your audience actually needs.
